text: Sing for Joy to God Our Strength

segments.text: Sing for joy to God our strength; make a joyful noise to the God of Jacob.
Lift up a song, strike the tambourine, play the sweet-sounding harp and lyre.
Sound the ram’s horn at the New Moon, and at the full moon on the day of our Feast.
For this is a statute for Israel, an ordinance of the God of Jacob.

He ordained it as a testimony for Joseph when he went out over the land of Egypt,
where I heard an unfamiliar language:
I relieved his shoulder of the burden; his hands were freed from the basket.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
You called out in distress, and I rescued you; I answered you from the cloud of thunder; 
I tested you at the waters of Meribah.  Selah
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Hear, O My people, and I will warn you: O Israel, if only you would listen to Me!
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: There must be no strange god among you, nor shall you bow to a foreign god.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I am the LORD your God, who brought you up out of Egypt. Open wide your mouth, and I will fill it.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But My people would not listen to Me, and Israel would not obey Me.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: So I gave them up to their stubborn heartsto follow their own devices.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: If only My people would listen to Me, if Israel would follow My ways,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: how soon I would subdue their enemies and turn My hand against their foes!
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Those who hate the LORD would feign obedience, and their doom would last forever.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But I would feed you the finest wheat; with honey from the rock I would satisfy you.
segments.speaker: God
